title: tithonia laciniata
intro: Tithonia laciniata, also known as Mexican sunflower, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America. It is a perennial herb used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant's leaves and flowers have been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. Tithonia laciniata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems.
